response.justification: The candidate is a strong web/full-stack engineer with broad experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no embedded networking or LAN-switch background, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, far below the strong C-for-UNIX proficiency required.
strengths: Extensive overall software development experience (15+ years) across many stacks
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux familiarity
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability — a mandatory native-level requirement
Lacks embedded networking, Linux kernel modification, and network security (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
C proficiency appears minimal and dated; career trajectory is firmly web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / security domain experience
